title: AI-to-Consumer business models
main_question: Can AI-to-C products become durable standalone businesses, or do they need large-company subsidy, bundled distribution, or non-consumer revenue to survive?
scope_in: Consumer-facing AI assistants, companions, productivity, search, creative, education, and personal-agent products
Products sold directly to individuals, even when owned by large B2B or platform companies
Hybrid products where consumer adoption supports enterprise, ads, APIs, hardware, or ecosystem retention
scope_out: Pure enterprise AI agents with no direct consumer product
Infrastructure-only model providers unless their consumer product affects the business model
Technical model benchmarking without business-model relevance

weekly: AI model cost pricing consumer app

title: SEA's AI initiatives, especially Shopee and agentic commerce direction
main_question: Is SEA building a durable, owned AI/agentic-commerce advantage for Shopee, or is its agentic-commerce direction dependent on external AI platforms (OpenAI, Google)?
scope_in: Sea Limited AI across Shopee, Monee, and Garena
Agentic commerce: AI product discovery and purchase completion
Seller-side AI tooling and ShopeeVIP AI bundling
SEA in-house AI capability (AI CoE, Codex)
scope_out: Non-SEA SEA-region AI efforts except as competitive comparison
Model-provider infrastructure economics unless they change Shopee's position
